extraction.one_line_summary: CORTINA Ivan sprinted from distance taking advantage of Hofstetter-Ewing crash at 150m that eliminated Bennett and Ewan
tactical_patterns: Decuninck-QuickStep aggressively forced crosswind splits post-intermediate sprint at 19km despite headwind forecast, creating sustained tension.
Cofidis leadout for Viviani completely invisible in finale, suggesting poor positioning discipline despite having dedicated train.
Cortina launched sprint at 150m—unusually early—but chaos from crash gave him clear road to hold power.
Sagan positioned perfectly pre-crash, suggesting his classics positioning instincts trump pure sprinter trains on technical finishes.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: eliminated by Hofstetter crash at 150m, hand injury with blood visible, finish uncertain
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: taken out by Hofstetter bike tangle despite Lotto-Soudal controlling into final 2km
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: second place despite no leadout, positioning judgment superior on chaotic technical finish
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: invisible with Cofidis leadout, sprint timing poor waiting until 150m then missing chaos advantage
rider_signals.CORTINA Ivan: positive: recognized crash chaos instantly, committed to early 150m sprint with confidence to hold power
rider_signals.HOFSTETTER Hugo: negative: bike tangled with Ewing wheel, caused crash eliminating sprint favorites
rider_signals.ALVAREZ Julián: negative: worst injured in earlier crash, finished last on stage
